title: Use of zirconium salts as scale inhibitor squeeze enhancers
abstract: Methods can include contacting an aqueous fluid including a zirconium salt with a scale inhibitor in a subterranean formation to form a zirconium salt/scale inhibitor complex, and systems related thereto.
claims:
Claims Atty Docket No.2023INV111606WO01-53501 CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: contacting an aqueous fluid comprising a zirconium salt with a scale inhibitor in a subterranean formation to form a zirconium salt/scale inhibitor complex. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the zirconium salt comprises zirconium perchlorate, zirconium acetate, zirconium butoxide, zirconium chloride, zirconium nitrate, zirconium lactate, or a combination thereof. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the zirconium salt comprises a zirconium lactate of formula: O Zr 4+ 4 . 4. the aqueous fluid further comprises fresh water, salt water, brine, seawater, or a combination thereof. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid further comprises another salt, a surfactant, an acid, a buffer, a diverting agent, a fluid loss control additive, a gas, a surface modifying agent, a tackifying agent, a foamer, a corrosion inhibitor, a catalyst, a clay control agent, a biocide, a friction reducer, an antifoam agent, a bridging agent, a flocculant, a H 2 S scavenger, an oxygen scavenger, a lubricant, a relative permeability modifier, a wetting agent, a filter cake removal agent, an antifreeze agent, or a combination thereof. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid further comprises another salt, a surfactant, or a combination thereof. 

Atty Docket No.2023INV111606WO01-53501 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the aqueous fluid comprises another salt comprising potassium chloride. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid is introduced before the scale inhibitor into the subterranean formation. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid is introduced simultaneously with the scale inhibitor into the subterranean formation. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid is introduced after the scale inhibitor into the subterranean formation. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid comprises at least about 1%, by weight, of the zirconium salt based on total weight of the aqueous fluid. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the aqueous fluid comprises about 0.1% to about 5%, by weight, of the zirconium salt based on total weight of the aqueous fluid. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the scale inhibitor comprises a phosphonate scale inhibitor, a polyacrylate scale inhibitor, a sulfonated polymer scale inhibitor, a solid scale inhibitor, or a combination thereof. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the scale inhibitor comprises a phosphonate scale inhibitor or a polymeric scale inhibitor. 15. A method for enhancing a scale inhibition treatment, comprising: mixing a base fluid and a zirconium salt to form an aqueous fluid; placing the aqueous fluid downhole to contact a subterranean formation; and afterwards placing a scale inhibitor fluid downhole to contact the aqueous fluid forming a zirconium salt/scale inhibitor complex. 

Atty Docket No.2023INV111606WO01-53501 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the zirconium salt comprises a zirconium lactate. 17. A method for enhancing production from a well site, comprising: detecting equal to or greater than about 1%, by volume, water in a produced hydrocarbon fluid; placing a scale inhibitor and an aqueous fluid comprising a zirconium salt downhole; and contacting the aqueous fluid comprising the zirconium salt with the scale inhibitor in a subterranean formation to form a zirconium salt/scale inhibitor complex for inhibiting scale formation. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the zirconium salt comprises zirconium lactate. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising placing the aqueous fluid downhole at a pressure less than the fracture gradient of the subterranean formation. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the fracture gradient of the subterranean formation is at least about 20,000 psi.
